name: Douglas County Housing Partnership
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counseling agency in Douglas County, Colorado offering free homebuyer education, down payment assistance, and foreclosure mediation to residents.
description_long:
Douglas County Housing Partnership (DCHP) is a multi-jurisdictional Housing Authority established in 2003 as a collaborative effort between local government, county government, and businesses to address affordable housing shortages in the Douglas County area. The organization is structured as a public corporation and political subdivision of Colorado, with financial support from the City of Lone Tree, Town of Castle Rock, Town of Parker, City of Castle Pines, and Douglas County itself.

DCHP operates as a HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency and provides a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost housing services. Their core offerings include down payment assistance programs for first-time homebuyers, home buyer education classes, amortizing loan programs, shared equity programs, rental assistance, foreclosure mediation counseling, and reverse mortgage (HECM) counseling. Since 2005, the organization has distributed over $6.5 million in down payment assistance to eligible borrowers.

The organization distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure, government backing, and direct integration with local jurisdictions across Douglas County. Unlike for-profit lenders or national servicers, DCHP's mission explicitly centers on affordability and access for working residents. Their programs are designed to remove barriers to homeownership rather than generate profit, and they provide education-first services to help consumers make informed decisions.

DCHP is genuinely non-profit and HUD-certified, making it a legitimate resource for Douglas County residents seeking housing assistance without predatory terms or hidden fees. The primary limitation is geographic: services are restricted to specific Douglas County jurisdictions. Additionally, the website provides limited detail about program eligibility requirements, income limits, or application processes, requiring potential applicants to contact the organization directly for specifics.
pros: HUD-approved Housing Counseling Agency with formal federal certification
Distributed over $6.5 million in down payment assistance since 2005, demonstrating sustained commitment
Operates as registered non-profit with government backing from five local/county jurisdictions
Provides free homebuyer education classes and no-cost foreclosure mediation counseling
Offers specialized reverse mortgage (HECM) counseling for seniors exploring home equity options
Multi-program approach including shared equity and amortizing loan options beyond down payment help
Located in Lone Tree, Colorado with established community presence since 2003
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Douglas County jurisdictions only
Website lacks specific details on program eligibility, income limits, and application timelines
No visible information about approval rates, average assistance amounts, or loan terms
Minimal transparency regarding staff qualifications or counselor certifications beyond HUD approval
No online application portal or intake system visible; requires direct contact for all inquiries
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Douglas County seeking down payment assistance without predatory lending
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need HUD-approved mediation and financial counseling
Seniors exploring reverse mortgage options and needing objective HECM counseling
Renters in Douglas County jurisdictions seeking rental assistance programs
diagnosis: Douglas County Housing Partnership is ideal for Douglas County residents—particularly first-time homebuyers and homeowners in foreclosure—seeking legitimate, government-backed housing assistance without profit-driven motives. The main caveat is geographic restriction; services only apply to residents within Lone Tree, Castle Rock, Parker, Castle Pines, and Douglas County proper, and applicants must initiate contact directly since the website omits specific eligibility and application details.
services: Down payment assistance programs for first-time homebuyers
Free homebuyer education and pre-purchase counseling classes
Amortizing loan programs for home purchases
Foreclosure mediation and loss mitigation counseling
Home equity mortgage conversion (HECM) reverse mortgage counseling
Community resource referrals and partner network coordination
Approved lender network and financing partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 9350 Heritage Hills Circle, Lone Tree, CO 80124
